report_name: Gartner Highlights AI, Deepfakes, and Supply Chain Attacks as Top Cybersecurity Threats for 2026-2027

attack_patterns.name: AI Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: Attackers target the software supply chain, including CI/CD pipelines, open-source repositories, and code dependencies, with the risk being exacerbated by Generative AI tools that can introduce vulnerabilities.

attack_patterns.name: Generate Deepfakes
attack_patterns.description: The use of deepfake technology for identity impersonation to bypass biometric authentication, create convincing CEO fraud scams, and subvert identity verification in processes like hiring.

attack_patterns.name: LLM Prompt Injection
attack_patterns.description: Attackers craft malicious inputs (prompts) to cause a large language model to ignore its original instructions and perform unintended actions, such as revealing sensitive system information or executing malicious code.

attack_patterns.name: Poison AI Model
attack_patterns.description: As part of AI application compromise, attackers can poison models to produce malicious outputs, one of the methods to exploit AI applications.
